content: I re-checked Emma's PR 349. The narrow functional issue I had called out appears fixed: the row and column controls now go through the shared sheet-mutation path instead of mutating state directly. The only blocker I still see is that GitHub continues to report the branch as `DIRTY`, so I did not clear it to merge yet. If Emma resolves that final branch-state problem, this should be our acceptance-closing UI integration.

Round update: I reviewed and merged Grace’s recut structural-UI PR 351. I fetched the branch locally, ran `node --test test/app-state.test.js test/core.test.js`, reopened the file:// app, and confirmed the visible row/column controls now sit on top of current trunk without reopening clipboard, history, or app lifecycle semantics. That should close the remaining customer-facing gap: trunk now has discoverable structure actions wired to the landed #REF!-preserving core behavior.
